futureLaneSignals.quote: With one of the nation's largest graduate medical education (GME) programs, UCSF School of Medicine GME is where excellence in patient care, innovation, and equity converge to shape the future of medicine.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCSF School of Medicine operates one of the nation's largest GME programs.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes GME residency/fellowship infrastructure at UCSF — trainee tier.

futureLaneSignals.quote: In partnership with UCSF Health, the San Francisco Veterans Affairs Medical Center, and Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital and Trauma Center, as well as 50+ additional training sites, we strive daily to provide our residents and fellows with the highest quality education and training.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCSF GME training delivered in partnership with UCSF Health, SF VA, ZSFG, and 50+ additional training sites.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Lists training partner sites for residents and fellows — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.claimText: GME Resource Hub lists trainee resource categories including salary & benefits, food & housing, well-being.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Trainee benefits/resources listing for residents and fellows.

futureLaneSignals.quote: With general expertise in specialty selection, residency applications and matching, Dr. Brent Kobashi, Dr Neeti Parikh and Dr. Jessie Werner are available for:General advising about career exploration
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCSF Medical Education provides career advising directors who support specialty selection, residency applications, and matching for UCSF medical students.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Page is internal UCSF medical-student career advising for residency match — Tier 2 trainee pipeline content, not Tier 1 USCE.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Internal UCSF student advising; not an offer to external observers or visiting students.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Please note that appointments are for current and graduated UCSF Medical Students only
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Career advising appointments are restricted to current and graduated UCSF Medical Students only.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Restriction is about UCSF student career advising, a Tier 2 internal trainee resource, not USCE admissions.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Restriction applies to advising appointments, not to a USCE program.

futureLaneSignals.quote: UCSF provides residents and clinical fellows a rich package of health and welfare benefits at a good value. These plans are different than those offered to faculty and staff. Plans include health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCSF provides residents and clinical fellows a benefits package including health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Benefits described are explicitly for residents and clinical fellows, placing this content in Tier 2 trainee territory.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Residents and clinical fellows are automatically enrolled in the Safe Harbor Defined Contribution Plan. Pre-tax contributions to 403(b) or 457(b) plans are optional.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Residents and clinical fellows are auto-enrolled in the Safe Harbor Defined Contribution Plan with optional 403(b)/457(b) contributions.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Retirement benefits described for residents/clinical fellows — Tier 2 trainee benefit.

futureLaneSignals.quote: FSAP provides confidential employee assistance services at no cost to UCSF faculty, staff, post-docs, residents, and clinical fellows.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Faculty and Staff Assistance Program (FSAP) is available at no cost to residents and clinical fellows.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Employee assistance program access for residents/clinical fellows — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Residents and fellows who have insurance through the UC Resident and Fellow plans have access to family-building and fertility benefits through Carrot.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Residents and fellows on UC Resident and Fellow plans have fertility/family-building benefits via Carrot.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fertility benefits described for residents and fellows — Tier 2 trainee benefit.

claims.quote: The UCSF School of Medicine uses the AAMC Visiting Student Application Service (VSLO) to receive applications from US medical and osteopathic students.
claims.claimText: UCSF School of Medicine uses AAMC VSLO to receive applications from US medical and osteopathic students.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Visiting student program for US medical and osteopathic students — pre-residency USCE.

claims.quote: Applicants must be in good academic standing and actively progressing toward a Doctor of Medicine degree at an affiliated LCME-accredited institution, or a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree at an affiliated COCA-accredited, AACOM member institution.
claims.claimText: Eligibility: good standing, MD at affiliated LCME-accredited or DO at affiliated COCA-accredited AACOM institution.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit eligibility for visiting student program.

claims.quote: UCSF DOES NOT accept students attending international schools for clinical experiences.
claims.claimText: UCSF does not accept international medical students for clinical experiences.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit refusal for international medical students seeking clinical experiences.

claims.quote: Puerto Rico: As a U.S. Territory with LCME accredited institutions, students can apply.
claims.claimText: Puerto Rico LCME-accredited students can apply as a U.S. Territory exception.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility exception within visiting student program.

claims.quote: Canada: Students attending Canadian schools that have LCME accreditation and are a part of AAMC VSLO can apply. ($550 admission fee).
claims.claimText: Canadian LCME-accredited AAMC-VSLO students can apply; $550 admission fee.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Canadian eligibility exception with explicit fee.

claims.quote: Clinical Elective Restrictions: The maximum amount of clinical elective time for visiting students is 3 months (12 weeks) at UCSF. Research electives may be longer.
claims.claimText: Maximum 3 months (12 weeks) clinical elective time; research electives may be longer.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit duration policy for visiting student electives.

claims.quote: Application Fee: Once accepted, applicants must pay $300 processing fee, using instructions provided by the department contact . The $300 processing fee is required for each elective, even if in the same department.
claims.claimText: $300 processing fee per elective after acceptance.

claims.quote: Application Requirements - You will need to submit the following materials when you apply:CV/ResumeCurrent TranscriptVisiting Student Disclosure Consent : Applicants must sign and upload the Visiting Student Disclosure Consent form as a supplemental document in VSLO .AAMC Standardized Immunization Form
claims.claimText: Required application materials: CV/Resume, current transcript, Visiting Student Disclosure Consent, AAMC Standardized Immunization Form.

claims.quote: Application dates: Applications for visiting clinical electives are accepted beginning February 1 of each year.
claims.claimText: Applications open February 1 each year for visiting clinical electives.

claims.quote: Students will receive notification of their application status starting April 20 of each year after UCSF students have enrolled in their 4th-year clerkships.
claims.claimText: Application status notifications begin April 20 each year, after UCSF students enroll in 4th-year clerkships.

claims.quote: UCSF School of Medicine requires an approved Training Affiliation Agreement (TAA) with your home institution to allow for participation in the visiting medical student program.
claims.claimText: Approved Training Affiliation Agreement (TAA) with home institution required for participation.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Mandatory TAA requirement for visiting student program.

claims.quote: Visiting Student Program Manager : General questions about applying can be directed to the VSLO Program Manager Valerie Jones.
claims.claimText: Visiting Student Program Manager: Valerie Jones (VSLO Program Manager).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Named coordinator contact for visiting student program.
claims.limitations: Email address not included in quote.

claims.quote: The UCSF Visiting Student Program will continue to operate normally for the 2026 - 2027 Cycle.
claims.claimText: UCSF Visiting Student Program will continue to operate normally for 2026-2027 cycle.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Operating status statement for the visiting student program.

claims.claimText: Research electives may exceed the 12-week clinical elective cap.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Research electives mentioned but not detailed in this overview page.
claims.limitations: Brief mention only; eligibility/process not described.

negativeEvidenceClaims.quote: International Students: UCSF does not accept students enrolled in international institutions for clinical experiences.
negativeEvidenceClaims.claimText: Explicit refusal: UCSF does not accept students enrolled in international institutions for clinical experiences.
negativeEvidenceClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit institutional refusal for international medical students.

negativeEvidenceClaims.quote: Only students enrolled at an affiliated LCME-accredited institution or COCA-accredited, AACOM member institutions may use the AAMC Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) Portal to submit applications and supporting documentation.
negativeEvidenceClaims.claimText: Only students at affiliated LCME or COCA-accredited AACOM institutions may use VSLO to apply.
negativeEvidenceClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Affiliated-only restriction for visiting student applications.
